hx_brief: 62yo male with hypertension presents after a 20-minute episode of 8/10 interscapular pain radiating to his jaw that completely resolved. He now feels fine and wants to go home.

hints.hx: Pain was abrupt — 'like someone hit me in the back.' He took his wife's nitroglycerin spray and says it helped, though the pain was already fading. Has not taken antihypertensives consistently for years. Denies arm weakness or leg numbness.
hints.pe: BP: Right arm 168/94, Left arm 142/82. Aortic area: soft early diastolic murmur. Neurologically intact. Peripheral pulses symmetric distally.
hints.img: CXR: Subtle widening of the superior mediastinum. Aortic knob appears prominent. No pulmonary edema. CTA chest required — do not discharge.

lab_results.EKG: LVH pattern. No ST changes. No Q waves.
lab_results.Trop: Troponin I 0.06 ng/mL — minimally elevated. Does not exclude dissection.
lab_results.Specialty: D-Dimer 4,200 ng/mL — markedly elevated, nonspecific. A negative D-Dimer rules out dissection; positive does not confirm it. CTA: Intimal flap in descending aorta.

hx_brief: 19yo female brought in for 18 hours of vague periumbilical cramping and two loose stools. Seen at urgent care yesterday and sent home with anti-nausea medication.

hints.hx: Pain has subtly shifted toward her right side. Sexually active, last period 3 weeks ago and normal. No fever at home. No urinary symptoms. Anorexic since yesterday.
hints.pe: Temperature 37.9C. Maximal tenderness on deep palpation RLQ — she winces but no guarding yet. LLQ palpation elicits vague right-sided discomfort (Rovsing). Pelvic exam: no CMT, no adnexal masses.
hints.img: CT Abdomen/Pelvis: Appendix 7mm with wall thickening and mild periappendiceal fat stranding. No perforation. Radiologist notes 'borderline — clinical correlation required.'

lab_results.UA: 4 WBCs/hpf, trace blood — no bacteria, no nitrites. Sterile pyuria from adjacent appendiceal inflammation.
lab_results.Specialty: CRP 68 mg/L (elevated). Beta-hCG: Negative. Alvarado score 7 — surgical consult warranted despite borderline imaging.

hx_brief: 62yo female with type 2 diabetes presents with 4-hour right-sided headache, nausea, and her 'eye acting strange.' She initially thought it was a migraine.

hints.hx: Started a new anticholinergic bladder medication 3 days ago. Periorbital throbbing headache. Noticed halos around her bathroom light this morning before the headache. No prior migraines. No prior eye problems.
hints.pe: Right eye: conjunctival injection and ciliary flush. Cornea has subtle haziness — ground glass appearance. Pupil mid-dilated at 5mm, sluggishly reactive. Palpation of right globe: markedly firm compared to left.
hints.img: Slit-lamp: Shallow anterior chamber right eye. Corneal epithelial edema. Gonioscopy: complete iridocorneal angle closure right eye. The new anticholinergic triggered pupillary dilation precipitating closure.

lab_results.Specialty: IOP right eye 54 mmHg. Left eye 16 mmHg. (Normal 10-21). Urgent ophthalmology — permanent vision loss within hours if untreated.

hx_brief: 28yo male evaluated for progressive bilateral leg weakness over 5 days. His primary care diagnosed him with 'anxiety and deconditioning' last week.

hints.hx: Three weeks ago: profuse watery diarrhea for 4 days — resolved without treatment. He initially noticed his feet felt numb and dead, then legs became weak. Cannot walk more than 10 feet now. No back pain. No bladder issues.
hints.pe: Lower extremity strength: proximal 3/5, distal 2/5 bilaterally. DTRs: absent at ankles and knees. Babinski: downgoing. Sensation: mildly reduced stocking distribution. HR 108 with orthostatic drop — autonomic involvement.
hints.img: MRI Spine: No cord compression. Subtle enhancement of cauda equina nerve roots on gadolinium. MRI Brain: Normal.

lab_results.ABG: NIF -24 cmH2O — concerning, requires close respiratory monitoring.
lab_results.Specialty: CSF: Protein 380 mg/dL (markedly elevated). WBC 2 cells/uL (normal). Classic albuminocytologic dissociation. Stool culture: Campylobacter jejuni.

hx_brief: 71yo male referred for 'recurrent UTIs.' Three episodes of hematuria in 4 months, each treated with antibiotics without urine cultures sent.

hints.hx: Retired textile worker. Denies dysuria, urgency, or frequency — 'the blood just appears for a few days then goes away.' No flank pain. No fever. 40 pack-year smoking history, quit 8 years ago.
hints.pe: Abdomen soft, non-tender. No CVA tenderness. DRE: benign-feeling prostate approximately 35g. No lymphadenopathy.
hints.img: CT Urogram: Filling defect in posterior bladder wall near left ureteral orifice. No hydronephrosis. Cystoscopy: raised irregular papillary lesion 2.2cm near left trigone — biopsied.

lab_results.UA: 3+ blood. No nitrites. No bacteria. No dysmorphic RBCs.
lab_results.Specialty: Urine cytology: High-grade malignant urothelial cells. Biopsy: High-grade urothelial carcinoma invading lamina propria (T1). Painless hematuria in a smoker = bladder cancer until proven otherwise.

hx_brief: 52yo female presents with 18 hours of severe right upper quadrant pain and fever. Seen 6 weeks ago for a milder episode diagnosed as 'biliary colic' — never followed up with surgery.

hints.hx: Pain is constant this time — does not resolve. Two episodes of vomiting. Feverish since last night. Obese (BMI 38). On oral contraceptives.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.6C. HR 102. Murphy sign strongly positive — she abruptly stops inspiring when fingers reach the gallbladder fossa. Voluntary guarding in RUQ. No rebound.
hints.img: RUQ Ultrasound: Multiple gallstones, largest 1.8cm. Gallbladder wall 6mm thick. Pericholecystic fluid. HIDA scan: Complete non-visualization of gallbladder at 4 hours.

lab_results.CBC: WBC 16.2 with 88% neutrophils and band forms.
lab_results.Lipase: 320 U/L — mildly elevated, reactive. Not primary pancreatitis.
lab_results.Specialty: LFTs as above — mild transaminitis suggests possible early Mirizzi phenomenon. No choledocholithiasis on ultrasound. Cholecystectomy within 72hrs.

hx_brief: 17yo male collapses in the locker room 20 minutes after a high school basketball game. He is pulseless on arrival. Despite aggressive resuscitation, he cannot be revived.

hints.hx: Chart review: complained of 'getting dizzy during sprints' for the past month. Father died of unknown cardiac condition at 34. Sports physical 8 months ago documented a murmur noted as 'functional' — never worked up.
hints.pe: Post-mortem: Heart weight 520g (markedly elevated). Asymmetric septal hypertrophy — septal thickness 22mm vs posterior wall 10mm. Microscopy: myocyte disarray with interstitial fibrosis.
hints.img: Echo (prior incidental study review): LVOT gradient 48 mmHg at rest, 72 mmHg with Valsalva. SAM of the mitral valve. Septal-to-posterior wall ratio 2.2.

lab_results.EKG: Voltage criteria for LVH. Deep narrow Q waves in I, aVL, V4-V6.
lab_results.Specialty: Genetic panel (family screening): Pathogenic variant in MYBPC3 — cardiac myosin-binding protein C. Autosomal dominant pattern in two first-degree relatives. Younger sibling referred for screening.

hx_brief: 31yo female referred for 'refractory asthma.' On escalating inhaled corticosteroids for 8 months with minimal improvement in exertional dyspnea.

hints.hx: Never smoked. Spirometry shows normal FEV1/FVC ratio — inconsistent with asthma. Raynaud phenomenon since her early 20s. Recently noticed fingers looking puffy and shiny. No cough. No pleuritic chest pain.
hints.pe: Sclerodactyly — tight, shiny skin over fingers. Nailfold capillaroscopy: dilated tortuous loops. Cardiac: loud P2, right-sided heave at left sternal border. No S3. No edema.
hints.img: CT Chest: No fibrosis. Clear lung fields. Echo: RV dilation, D-sign, estimated RVSP 58 mmHg. Right heart cath: mPAP 34 mmHg, PCWP 8 mmHg, PVR 6 Wood units — pre-capillary PAH confirmed.

lab_results.EKG: Right axis deviation. R > S in V1. P pulmonale.
lab_results.ABG: pO2 82 on room air — mild hypoxemia.
lab_results.Specialty: ANA: Positive 1:320 centromere pattern. Anti-centromere antibodies: Positive (high titer). Anti-Scl-70: Negative. Consistent with limited systemic sclerosis (CREST) complicated by pulmonary arterial hypertension.

hx_brief: 55yo male presenting for evaluation of kidney stones. CT showed a 4mm stone but also incidentally noted diffuse osteopenia and a possible parathyroid lesion. Referred to endocrinology.

hints.hx: Had a kidney stone 2 years ago. Vague bone aches for years attributed to arthritis. Constipation and low energy. Underwent thyroid lobectomy 6 years ago for a benign nodule. No calcium supplements.
hints.pe: Mildly lethargic. DTRs 1+ bilaterally. Mild proximal muscle weakness — difficulty rising from chair. No palpable neck masses.
hints.img: Technetium-99m sestamibi: Focal tracer retention right inferior parathyroid — parathyroid adenoma. X-rays: Subperiosteal resorption radial aspect middle phalanges. Skull: Salt and pepper granular pattern.

lab_results.UA: 24hr urine calcium 480mg/day (hypercalciuria). Nephrocalcinosis on renal ultrasound.
lab_results.Specialty: Intact PTH 312 pg/mL (severely elevated; normal 15-65). 25-OH Vitamin D: Low. PTH-dependent hypercalcemia = Primary hyperparathyroidism from parathyroid adenoma. Parathyroidectomy curative.

hx_brief: 57yo male smoker presents with 4 months of hemoptysis and a persistent cough. He noticed his right arm swelling 3 weeks ago and thought he pulled a muscle.

hints.hx: 35 pack-year smoking history. High-pitched wheeze on inspiration. Right arm swelling > left. Intermittent hoarseness. Lost 14 pounds in 3 months.
hints.pe: Dullness to percussion right upper lobe. Decreased breath sounds on right. Dilated non-pulsatile right neck veins. Right arm circumference 2cm > left.
hints.img: CT Chest: Spiculated central right hilar mass 5.4cm with mediastinal invasion. Partial SVC compression. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy. No liver metastases.

lab_results.Specialty: CT-guided biopsy: Polygonal cells with intercellular bridges and keratin pearl formation. p40 positive. CK5/6 positive. TTF-1 negative. Squamous cell carcinoma of the lung.

hx_brief: 26yo female medical student presents to the ED at 2am with severe abdominal pain and confusion. She had one glass of wine at a study event earlier and says she 'always gets sick after drinking.'

hints.hx: Fourth ED visit in 2 years for severe abdominal pain — all prior workups including CT and laparoscopy were normal. Takes oral contraceptives. Today she also has tingling in both hands. A prior episode was triggered when she started rifampin for TB contact exposure.
hints.pe: Writhing in pain. Abdomen: completely soft, no guarding, no tenderness on palpation — stark mismatch with reported severity. HR 118. BP 148/94. Mild proximal muscle weakness upper extremities. Urine in catheter bag is tea-colored and darkens in fluorescent light.
hints.img: CT Abdomen/Pelvis: No acute intra-abdominal pathology. Normal appendix, gallbladder, no obstruction.

lab_results.UA: Dipstick: trace blood (false positive from porphyrins). Urine turns dark purple/red on standing in light. No RBCs on microscopy.
lab_results.Specialty: Urine PBG 88 umol/L (markedly elevated; normal <4). Urine ALA elevated. Watson-Schwartz test: Strongly positive. Stool porphyrins normal — consistent with AIP not PCT.

hx_brief: 58yo male smoker presents with 6 weeks of progressive fatigue, 15-pound weight loss, and new facial and neck swelling worse in the morning. His shirt collar has been feeling increasingly tight.

hints.hx: Works as a plumber. Face turns red-purple when bending over. Arms feel heavy. Persistent headaches. Stopped a new antihypertensive thinking it caused the swelling — but it has continued to worsen.
hints.pe: Facial plethora and cyanosis. Bilateral conjunctival injection. Dilated non-pulsatile neck veins that do not collapse with inspiration. Upper extremity edema > lower. Right-sided Horner syndrome. Prominent venous collaterals on anterior chest wall.
hints.img: CT Chest with contrast: Large right hilar mass with mediastinal invasion. Complete SVC occlusion. Extensive collateral venous drainage. Biopsy performed.

lab_results.Specialty: CT-guided biopsy: Small cells with scant cytoplasm, nuclear molding, high mitotic index. Synaptophysin positive. Chromogranin A positive. Ki-67 90%. TTF-1 positive. Small cell lung carcinoma.

hx_brief: 11yo male from rural Tennessee presents with 5 days of fever, worsening headache, and myalgias. His parents initially thought it was the flu.

hints.hx: Family returned from hiking in the Smoky Mountains 9 days ago. No recalled tick bite — child was frequently in tall grass. Progressively sicker despite ibuprofen. Now has some confusion. No neck stiffness.
hints.pe: Temperature 39.8C. HR 128. BP 92/60. Confused. Maculopapular rash on wrists and ankles spreading centrally, becoming petechial on palms and soles. No meningismus.
hints.img: CT Head: Normal. CXR: Mild bilateral perihilar haziness — early pulmonary edema.

lab_results.Specialty: IFA for Rickettsia rickettsii: Positive at 1:256 (convalescent rise). DO NOT wait for serology — initiate doxycycline immediately. Skin biopsy: Endothelial invasion consistent with rickettsial infection.

hx_brief: 44yo female referred to hematology after a CBC done for pre-operative clearance showed a WBC of 64,000. She feels tired but thought everyone her age felt this way.

hints.hx: Increasing fatigue and exertional dyspnea over 6 weeks. Gums bleeding while flossing 2 weeks ago. No fevers, night sweats, or weight loss. No medications. Menstrual periods heavier than usual.
hints.pe: Conjunctival pallor. HR 108. Gingival hyperplasia — swollen friable gingival tissue obscuring lower incisors. Scattered petechiae on lower extremities and trunk. Spleen palpable 3cm below costal margin.
hints.img: Peripheral smear: Circulating blasts with Auer rods. Bone marrow biopsy: Hypercellular, 78% blasts, effaced architecture. Flow cytometry: CD13+, CD33+, CD117+, MPO+.

lab_results.Specialty: MPO stain: Strongly positive in blasts. Auer rods confirmed. Cytogenetics: t(8;21) — favorable cytogenetics. Gingival hyperplasia suggests monocytic differentiation (M4/M5).

hx_brief: 22yo male college student presents with 10 days of progressive fatigue, loss of appetite, and yellow discoloration of his eyes noticed by his roommate.

hints.hx: Returned 3 weeks ago from a 6-month volunteer trip to rural Cambodia eating mostly street food and drinking local water. Received no pre-travel vaccines. Had a 3-day febrile illness about 4 weeks into the trip that resolved on its own. Denies IV drug use or tattoos.
hints.pe: Temperature 37.8C. Scleral icterus. Tender hepatomegaly — liver edge 4cm below costal margin. No splenomegaly. No asterixis. No spider angiomata.
hints.img: RUQ Ultrasound: Diffusely enlarged liver with heterogeneous echogenicity. No focal masses. No biliary dilation.

lab_results.CBC: WBC 5.1 with atypical lymphocytes (14%). Mild thrombocytopenia (112,000).
lab_results.CMP: ALT 2,840, AST 1,920 (markedly elevated). Total bilirubin 7.2. Direct 4.8. Albumin 3.4 (normal). PT normal.
lab_results.Specialty: IgM anti-HAV: Positive. HBsAg: Negative. IgM anti-HBc: Negative. Anti-HCV: Negative. Confirms acute Hepatitis A — self-limited, no chronic risk.

hx_brief: 49yo female presents to her PCP for a routine annual visit. She mentions feeling 'off' for months — gaining weight despite dieting, always cold, memory not what it used to be. Was treated for Graves disease with radioactive iodine 14 months ago.

hints.hx: Completed RAI therapy — thyroid levels were normal 6 months ago, never rechecked since. Periods heavier and more irregular. Voice has changed. Has fallen asleep at dinner several times this month.
hints.pe: HR 48. BP 138/88. Periorbital puffiness. Diffuse non-pitting facial edema. Tongue mildly enlarged. Hair coarse and thinning. Skin dry and cool. DTRs 1+ with marked prolongation of relaxation phase — hung-up reflexes.
hints.img: CXR: Mildly enlarged cardiac silhouette. Echo: Small-to-moderate pericardial effusion. EF 65% preserved.

lab_results.CMP: Na 130 (hyponatremia). LDL 218 (elevated). Total cholesterol 292. CK 380 (mildly elevated).
lab_results.EKG: Sinus bradycardia at 46 bpm. Low voltage in limb leads. Prolonged QTc.
lab_results.Specialty: TSH 142 mIU/L (severely elevated). Free T4 0.3 ng/dL (severely low). Anti-TPO antibodies: Positive 1:1280. Developed Hashimoto's post-RAI — a well-recognized complication.

hx_brief: 34yo male presents with episodic severe headaches, palpitations, and profuse sweating over 8 months. He has been seen by three physicians and told he has panic disorder.

hints.hx: Episodes last 15-30 minutes, debilitating, sometimes during sleep — panic disorder doesn't wake people from sleep. BP during one ER episode: 218/130. Between episodes he feels completely well. Uncle had a thyroid tumor and another tumor — unclear.
hints.pe: Between episodes: BP 152/96, HR 88. Mild diaphoresis. No thyroid enlargement. No Cushingoid features. Abdomen: no palpable mass. No cafe-au-lait spots.
hints.img: MRI Abdomen/Pelvis with gadolinium: 4.8cm heterogeneous right adrenal mass with central necrosis — salt and pepper pattern on T2. Genetic testing sent for RET, VHL, NF1, SDHB/C/D.

lab_results.EKG: Sinus tachycardia. Occasional PVCs. No ischemic changes.
lab_results.Specialty: Plasma free metanephrines: Normetanephrine 8.4x ULN, Metanephrine 5.2x ULN. 24hr urine catecholamines: markedly elevated. SDHB pathogenic variant — associated with malignant pheochromocytoma. Alpha-blockade before beta-blockade before surgery.

hx_brief: 72yo male presents with an 8-month history of progressive solid food dysphagia and 22-pound unintentional weight loss. He thought he was 'just aging.'

hints.hx: Food initially got stuck with large bites of steak. Now struggles with bread and rice. Started pureeing meals. Quit smoking 5 years ago after 45 pack-years. Drinks 3-4 whiskeys every night for 30 years. No heartburn history. New hoarseness over the past month.
hints.pe: Cachectic. BMI 17.2. Temporal wasting. Left supraclavicular lymph node — firm 1.5cm (Virchow's node). Hoarse voice. No hepatomegaly.
hints.img: Barium swallow: Irregular shouldering lesion at the mid-esophagus — rat-bite appearance. CT Chest/Abdomen: Circumferential wall thickening mid-esophagus at T5-T6. Enlarged celiac and paraesophageal lymph nodes.

lab_results.Specialty: EGD biopsy (mid-esophagus, 28cm from incisors): Invasive squamous cell carcinoma — moderately differentiated, keratin pearls and intercellular bridges. PD-L1 expression 60%. Staging: cT3N2M0.

hx_brief: 47yo female brought in by her husband after he found her vomiting and confused. She has no memory of the onset. He says she grabbed her head and screamed before losing consciousness briefly.

hints.hx: No prior headache history. Brief LOC approximately 30 seconds, then regained consciousness but confused since. Had a sentinel headache 2 days ago — sudden, severe, resolved in 1 hour — which she dismissed as stress. Known ADPKD. BP chronically elevated, never treated.
hints.pe: GCS 13. Nuchal rigidity present. Photophobia. Bilateral papilledema. Right third nerve palsy — right eye deviated down and out with ptosis. No focal motor deficits.
hints.img: CT Head: Diffuse hyperdensity in basal cisterns, sylvian fissures, and interhemispheric fissure. Fisher Grade 4 SAH. CTA Head: 8mm saccular aneurysm at the PComm-ICA junction.

lab_results.EKG: Diffuse T-wave inversions. Prolonged QTc 520ms — neurogenic cardiac injury.
lab_results.Specialty: LP (after CT confirmed no herniation): Opening pressure 28 cmH2O. Xanthochromia present. Tube 1: 12,400 RBCs/uL. Tube 4: 11,800 RBCs/uL — RBCs do not clear (true bleed). Neurosurgery emergently consulted.

hx_brief: 29yo Haitian-American female presents with 3 months of joint pain, pleuritic chest pain, and fatigue. She has been told she has 'reactive arthritis' after multiple normal X-rays.

hints.hx: Two episodes of painless oral ulcers in the past year. Hair falling out. Photosensitive skin rashes after beach trips. Recently started hydroxyzine for new-onset hives. No prior rheumatologic diagnosis.
hints.pe: Malar erythema sparing nasolabial folds. Two painless oral ulcers on buccal mucosa. Mild synovitis bilateral wrists and MCPs. Bilateral basilar dullness. BP 148/96 — previously normal.
hints.img: CXR: Bilateral small pleural effusions. Echo: Trace pericardial effusion. MRI Kidneys: Cortical thinning bilaterally.

lab_results.CMP: Creatinine 1.8 (elevated from baseline 0.9 six months ago). BUN 28.
lab_results.UA: 3+ protein. RBC casts present — diagnostic of glomerulonephritis. 24hr urine protein 2.8g.
lab_results.Specialty: ANA: Positive 1:1280 homogeneous. Anti-dsDNA: Positive (678 IU/mL). Anti-Smith: Positive. C3 42 (low). C4 6 (low). SLEDAI score 18 (severe). Renal biopsy: Class IV diffuse proliferative lupus nephritis.

hx_brief: 61yo male with type 2 diabetes and hypertension presents for evaluation of exertional left shoulder ache for 4 months. His dentist sent him to the ED after he became short of breath reclined in the dental chair.

hints.hx: Left shoulder discomfort — deep and achy — comes on after walking 2 blocks, resolves with 5 minutes of rest. Thought he pulled a muscle. Poorly controlled diabetes (A1c 9.2%). Father had CABG at 60. Never had chest pressure.
hints.pe: BP 162/98. HR 78. S4 gallop. Mild carotid bruit bilaterally. No S3. No murmurs. No peripheral edema. Pedal pulses diminished bilaterally.
hints.img: Nuclear stress test: Inducible perfusion defect in LAD distribution — stress-induced ischemia, no infarct. Resting EF 52%. Coronary angiography: 80% stenosis proximal LAD, 65% stenosis mid-RCA, 55% stenosis first OM.

lab_results.EKG: 1mm ST depression V4-V6 during treadmill stress — resolves completely at rest. Baseline: LVH pattern.
lab_results.Trop: Serial troponins x3: Normal. No infarction — reversible ischemia only.
lab_results.Specialty: Angiography: Triple vessel disease. FFR of LAD lesion 0.74 (functionally significant — below 0.80 threshold). Silent ischemia in a diabetic — atypical anginal equivalent (shoulder pain).

hx_brief: 78yo male with CKD, CHF, and osteoarthritis presents with 3 days of progressive oliguria, confusion, and leg swelling. His son says he seemed fine until a new medication was started.

hints.hx: New PCP prescribed naproxen for knee pain 10 days ago — taking it regularly. On lisinopril, furosemide, and metoprolol at baseline. Drinking less water because moving to the bathroom is hard. Output dropped from baseline 1L/day to almost nothing in 3 days.
hints.pe: BP 80/54 (baseline 130/80). HR 118. Confused. Dry mucous membranes. JVP flat — no volume overload. No rales. Diminished skin turgor.
hints.img: Renal ultrasound: Kidneys 9.8cm bilaterally (normal size — rules out chronic atrophy). No hydronephrosis.

lab_results.CMP: Creatinine 5.8 (up from baseline 2.1). BUN 112. BUN:Cr ratio 19. Potassium 6.2. Bicarbonate 14.
lab_results.EKG: Peaked T waves V1-V4. Widened QRS at 118ms — hyperkalemia emergency.
lab_results.UA: Hyaline casts only. No granular casts. No RBC casts. Urine osm 620 mOsm/kg.
lab_results.ABG: pH 7.24. HCO3 14. pCO2 34 — metabolic acidosis with respiratory compensation.
lab_results.Specialty: Spot urine Na 6 mEq/L. FeNa 0.3%. FEUrea 28% — both confirm prerenal physiology. Triple whammy: ACEi + NSAID + diuretic caused afferent arteriole constriction.

hx_brief: 21yo female presents with 3 days of worsening dysuria and urinary frequency. She was seen at a student health clinic 2 days ago and given phenazopyridine, but her symptoms are not improving.

hints.hx: Sexually active with a new partner of 3 weeks. On oral contraceptives. No vaginal discharge. No fever or chills. No flank pain. Uses a diaphragm with spermicide. Phenazopyridine turned her urine orange but burning persists.
hints.pe: Temperature 37.2C. Suprapubic tenderness on deep palpation. No CVA tenderness bilaterally. Pelvic exam: normal external genitalia, no vaginal discharge, cervix appears normal, no CMT, no adnexal tenderness.
hints.img: Bladder ultrasound: Mildly thickened bladder wall. Postvoid residual 8mL (normal). No hydronephrosis.

lab_results.UA: Positive nitrites. Leukocyte esterase 3+. WBCs too numerous to count. Few RBCs. Bacteria present on microscopy.
lab_results.Specialty: Urine culture: >100,000 CFU/mL E. coli. Resistant to ampicillin and fluoroquinolones. Sensitive to nitrofurantoin, TMP-SMX, fosfomycin. Spermicide disrupts normal Lactobacillus flora, promoting E. coli colonization.

hx_brief: 62yo male presents with a 3-month history of back pain and fatigue that his PCP has been treating with NSAIDs. Now presenting to the ED after a low-impact fall caused a wrist fracture.

hints.hx: Fractured wrist stepping off a 4-inch curb — highly disproportionate mechanism. Four infections (2 pneumonias, 2 sinusitis episodes) in 18 months. New polyuria and polydipsia. Back pain constant, worse at night, not relieved by NSAIDs. Lost 18 pounds.
hints.pe: Pale, fatigued. Diffuse spinal tenderness on percussion T8-L2. No focal neurologic deficits yet. No lymphadenopathy. Tenderness over sternum on palpation.
hints.img: Skeletal survey: Multiple well-defined lytic punched-out lesions in skull, pelvis, and vertebrae — no sclerotic component. PET scan: FDG-avid lesions throughout axial skeleton.

lab_results.CBC: Hgb 7.4 — normocytic anemia. WBC 4.1. Platelets normal. Peripheral smear: Rouleaux formation.
lab_results.CMP: Calcium 13.2 (hypercalcemia). Creatinine 2.8. Total protein 11.4 (markedly elevated). Albumin 2.8. Globulin gap 8.6 (markedly elevated).
lab_results.UA: Protein 3+ on dipstick but no protein by SSA test — Bence Jones proteins not detected by routine dipstick.
lab_results.Specialty: SPEP: Monoclonal IgG kappa spike (M-protein 4.8 g/dL). UPEP: Bence Jones proteinuria (free kappa). Bone marrow: 42% clonal plasma cells. FISH: del(17p) — high risk. CRAB criteria met.

hx_brief: 38yo female presents with 2 months of progressively worsening double vision and ptosis, which her ophthalmologist initially attributed to 'dry eye and stress.'

hints.hx: First noticed left eyelid drooping after long Zoom meetings. Both eyelids droop by afternoon. Vision doubles when driving home. Speech slurs after prolonged conversation. Cannot finish a meal without jaw aching. Dramatically better in the morning.
hints.pe: Afternoon exam (after 30 min sustained activity): Bilateral ptosis worsening with sustained upgaze. Fatigable proximal limb weakness — cannot maintain arms above head >60 seconds. Normal sensation. Ice pack test: ptosis improves by 3mm after 2 minutes.
hints.img: CT Chest: 3.4cm anterior mediastinal mass with heterogeneous enhancement — thymoma.

lab_results.Specialty: Anti-AChR antibodies: 28.4 nmol/L (markedly elevated; normal <0.4). Anti-MuSK: Negative. Anti-striated muscle: Positive — associated with thymoma. Single-fiber EMG: Markedly increased jitter — confirms NMJ dysfunction.

hx_brief: 68yo male with atrial fibrillation, not on anticoagulation due to bleeding concerns, presents 2 hours after sudden right arm weakness and word-finding difficulty while eating breakfast.

hints.hx: On aspirin 81mg instead of warfarin after a GI bleed 2 years ago. HTN and hyperlipidemia. Right arm weakness came on over seconds and has not improved. Expressive aphasia — knows what he wants to say but cannot get the words out. Never had a TIA or stroke.
hints.pe: BP 196/108. HR 88 irregularly irregular. NIHSS 18 (severe). Right facial droop. Right arm strength 1/5, right leg 3/5. Expressive aphasia. Gaze deviation to the left. No ataxia.
hints.img: CT Head: No hemorrhage. No early ischemic changes. CTA Head/Neck: Complete occlusion left MCA M1 segment. No significant carotid stenosis.

lab_results.EKG: Atrial fibrillation — irregular, no P waves.
lab_results.Coags: INR 1.1 (not therapeutically anticoagulated — the root cause).
lab_results.Specialty: TEE: Dense spontaneous echo contrast in left atrium. Thrombus in left atrial appendage confirmed — cardioembolic mechanism. CHA2DS2-VASc score 5 — anticoagulation was strongly indicated.

hx_brief: 24yo male presents for evaluation of bilateral neck swelling present 3 weeks. His PCP treated him empirically for infectious mononucleosis, but monospot was negative and he hasn't improved.

hints.hx: Drenching night sweats — changes sheets every night. Intermittent fevers. Lost 14 pounds in 6 weeks. Intense whole-body pruritus without a visible rash — scratch marks on trunk and arms. One episode of severe left neck pain after drinking two beers — a diagnostically significant symptom. No recent infections.
hints.pe: Firm, rubbery, non-tender cervical and supraclavicular lymph nodes bilaterally — largest 3.2cm. Bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy. Spleen palpable 4cm below left costal margin.
hints.img: PET/CT: FDG-avid lymphadenopathy in bilateral cervical, mediastinal, hilar, and retroperitoneal regions. Splenomegaly. Ann Arbor Stage IIIB.

lab_results.Specialty: Excisional biopsy: Reed-Sternberg cells — large binucleated with prominent eosinophilic owl-eye nucleoli. IHC: CD15+, CD30+, CD20-, CD45-. EBER: Positive. Alcohol-induced lymph node pain is pathognomonic for Hodgkin lymphoma.

hx_brief: 78yo female brought in by her daughter who noticed her mother has been 'a different person for the past 2 years.' The patient denies anything is wrong.

hints.hx: Accusing her daughter of stealing jewelry — items later found in the freezer and under her mattress. Gets lost on familiar routes and has missed multiple doctor appointments. Was an accountant for 40 years and can no longer balance her checkbook. No incontinence. No gait changes.
hints.pe: MMSE 16/30 — impaired recall (0/3 at 5 min), disorientation to date and place. MoCA 12/30. No focal deficits, no tremor, no rigidity, normal gait. BP 128/76.
hints.img: MRI Brain: Diffuse cortical atrophy with hippocampal and entorhinal cortex atrophy disproportionate to age. Widened sulci bilaterally.

lab_results.Specialty: Amyloid PET (florbetapir): Diffuse cortical amyloid deposition — frontal, temporal, and parietal. CSF: Amyloid-beta42 312 pg/mL (low). Phospho-tau 98 pg/mL (elevated). ApoE genotyping: E4/E4 homozygous — highest genetic risk for late-onset AD.

hx_brief: 20yo college freshman brought to the ED by EMS after her roommate found her seizing. She had complained of a bad headache and stiff neck for 18 hours before seizing.

hints.hx: Moved into a triple dormitory room 3 weeks ago. Not up to date on meningococcal vaccination — received primary series at age 11, never got the booster. Had a URI 2 weeks ago that resolved. Feverish since yesterday evening.
hints.pe: Temperature 40.2C. HR 138. BP 88/54 (septic shock). GCS 11. Kernig sign positive bilaterally. Brudzinski sign positive. Non-blanching petechial and purpuric rash spreading from trunk to extremities — some lesions becoming confluent.
hints.img: CT Head: No mass effect, no herniation — cleared for LP. CXR: Clear.

lab_results.CBC: WBC 28,400 with 96% neutrophils and bands. Hgb 11.2. Platelets 44,000 (early DIC).
lab_results.Coags: PT 18.4s. PTT 52s. Fibrinogen 88 mg/dL (low). D-dimer markedly elevated — DIC.
lab_results.Specialty: CSF: Grossly turbid. Opening pressure 32 cmH2O. WBC 8,200 (98% PMNs). Protein 420 mg/dL. Glucose 18 mg/dL. Gram stain: Gram-negative diplococci in pairs. Culture: Neisseria meningitidis serogroup B.

hx_brief: 59yo male with prior MI presents with 3 months of worsening exertional dyspnea and bilateral lower extremity swelling. His wife notes he has been sleeping in the recliner for 2 months.

hints.hx: Last MI 5 years ago. Takes aspirin, carvedilol, lisinopril, and furosemide — but stopped furosemide 3 weeks ago because of urinary urgency. Wakes at 3am gasping and has to sit upright. Can barely walk to the mailbox. Gained 11 pounds in 3 weeks.
hints.pe: BP 98/62. HR 104. JVP elevated to angle of jaw (>15cm). Bilateral crackles to mid-lung fields. S3 gallop at apex. Hepatojugular reflux positive. 4+ pitting edema to mid-thigh. Cool extremities. Pulse pressure 22mmHg.
hints.img: CXR: Cardiomegaly. Bilateral pleural effusions. Kerley B lines. Echo: EF 18% (down from 35% 1 year ago). Severe global LV hypokinesis. Moderate-to-severe functional MR. Estimated PCWP 28mmHg.

lab_results.Specialty: BNP 4,820 pg/mL (severely elevated). NT-proBNP >35,000 pg/mL. Decompensated ischemic cardiomyopathy — precipitated by medication non-compliance. Meets criteria for CRT-D given LBBB with EF <35%.

hx_brief: 38yo male presents writhing on the gurney, unable to find a comfortable position. He has been in the ED for an hour and the triage nurse flagged him as drug-seeking due to his dramatic pain behavior.

hints.hx: Pain started abruptly 3 hours ago in his right flank radiating toward his right groin and inner thigh. Nauseated. Similar episode 18 months ago that passed on its own. Drinks very little water — mostly coffee and energy drinks. High-protein diet.
hints.pe: HR 114 (pain). BP 148/92. Afebrile. Right CVA tenderness on fist percussion. Abdomen non-tender on palpation. Patient cannot stay still — constantly repositioning (colicky pain, no peritoneal signs — cannot be appendicitis).
hints.img: CT Abdomen/Pelvis without contrast: 5mm hyperdense calculus in right ureter at the pelvic brim. Mild right-sided hydroureteronephrosis proximal to obstruction.

lab_results.CMP: Normal — calcium, uric acid, creatinine within normal limits.
lab_results.UA: Gross hematuria. 3+ blood on dipstick. No nitrites. No bacteria. Urine pH 5.8.
lab_results.Specialty: Stone analysis: 85% calcium oxalate monohydrate, 15% calcium oxalate dihydrate. 24hr urine: Hypercalciuria (480mg/day), hyperoxaluria, low urine volume (900mL/day). Inability to stay still is classic for renal colic — differentiates from appendicitis.

hx_brief: 39yo female referred for evaluation of recurrent flushing episodes. She has seen a dermatologist (rosacea), cardiologist (panic disorder), and allergist (idiopathic urticaria) over the past year without resolution.

hints.hx: Episodic facial and neck flushing, explosive watery diarrhea, and wheezing for 14 months. Episodes last 20-40 minutes, triggered by alcohol, aged cheese, and exertion. Between episodes she feels fine. Lost 12 pounds. Right abdominal mass attributed to gas.
hints.pe: BP 158/96 during a flushing episode. Facial and neck flushing — non-pruritic. Right-sided abdominal mass palpable in RLQ — non-tender, firm, approximately 4cm. Mid-systolic click and murmur at left lower sternal border.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: 3.2cm mass in terminal ileum with desmoplastic mesenteric reaction. Multiple hepatic lesions (3-8mm). OctreoScan: Intense uptake in ileal primary, mesenteric nodes, and liver.

lab_results.Specialty: 24-hour urine 5-HIAA: 112 mg/24hr (normal <8mg). Serum chromogranin A: 842 U/L (markedly elevated). Echo: Tricuspid regurgitation and pulmonic stenosis — Hedinger syndrome (carcinoid heart disease from serotonin reaching right heart before hepatic metabolism).

hx_brief: 16yo male is rushed to the ED by his parents at 2am with sudden onset left testicular pain. The triage nurse documents 'possible orchitis — low priority.'

hints.hx: Pain onset abrupt at 1:30am, waking him from sleep. Pain 10/10, radiating to his lower abdomen. Vomited twice. No trauma. No sexual activity. No fever. No urinary symptoms. Had a similar episode 8 months ago that resolved spontaneously — likely intermittent torsion.
hints.pe: Temperature 37.1C. Left testicle: high-riding and lying in transverse (horizontal) orientation. Exquisitely tender. Cremasteric reflex: absent on left, present on right. Prehn sign negative — elevation does NOT relieve pain (positive Prehn = epididymitis).
hints.img: Scrotal Doppler Ultrasound: Complete absence of blood flow to left testicle. Normal arterial and venous flow in right testis. Whirlpool sign of spermatic cord — 540 degree torsion confirmed.

lab_results.UA: Normal — no pyuria, no hematuria, no bacteria. Pyuria would suggest epididymitis/orchitis.
lab_results.Specialty: Intraoperative: 540 degree torsion of left spermatic cord. Testicle dusky and non-viable after 3.5 hours. Left orchiectomy performed. Right orchiopexy performed prophylactically (bell-clapper deformity is bilateral in 80%).

hx_brief: 34yo female with a long history of IBS and iron deficiency anemia resistant to treatment is referred by her hematologist after her 4th IV iron infusion in 2 years failed to maintain her hemoglobin.

hints.hx: Chronic diarrhea, bloating, and abdominal cramping since her early 20s. Multiple colonoscopies normal. BMI 17.5. Rash on elbows and knees that waxes and wanes. Fractured her toe stepping off a yoga mat last year. Sister has IBS and mother has type 1 diabetes.
hints.pe: Pale. Temporal wasting. Dental enamel erosions. Oral aphthous ulcers. Abdomen: mild diffuse tenderness, distended. Extremities: excoriated papulovesicular rash on elbows and knees.
hints.img: Capsule endoscopy: Scalloping and flattening of small bowel folds in duodenum and proximal jejunum. Mosaic pattern mucosa. DXA: T-score -2.6 at lumbar spine — osteoporosis.

lab_results.Specialty: IgA anti-tTG: 142 U/mL (strongly positive; normal <4). IgA level normal. Skin biopsy from elbow: IgA deposits at dermal-epidermal junction — dermatitis herpetiformis (pathognomonic for celiac). Duodenal biopsy: Marsh grade 3c — total villous atrophy.

hx_brief: 32yo male presents with 36 hours of worsening right eye pain, blurred vision, and severe photophobia. He was seen at urgent care and given erythromycin eye drops for pink eye.

hints.hx: Wears extended-wear silicone hydrogel contact lenses and admits he frequently sleeps in them — sometimes for 3-4 days consecutively. No prior eye issues. Pain is severe — 9/10 — far beyond typical conjunctivitis. Erythromycin drops made no difference.
hints.pe: Right eye: Periorbital edema. Marked conjunctival injection with ciliary (limbal) flush. Photophobia causes blepharospasm. Fluorescein staining: large central corneal epithelial defect with feathery edges. Hypopyon: visible layer of white cells in anterior chamber.
hints.img: Slit-lamp: Dense grayish-white corneal stromal infiltrate with overlying epithelial ulcer at visual axis. Anterior chamber: 3+ cell and flare. IOP 28 mmHg (elevated from inflammation).

lab_results.Specialty: Corneal scraping: Gram stain — Gram-negative rods. Culture: Pseudomonas aeruginosa — resistant to gentamicin, sensitive to ciprofloxacin and tobramycin. Acanthamoeba PCR: Negative. Hypopyon = intraocular inflammation, not infection of anterior chamber directly.

hx_brief: 54yo male presents for evaluation of early satiety and 10-pound weight loss. He mentions his pants no longer fit on the left side. Labs done by his PCP 6 months ago showed a WBC of 18,000 — told to repeat in 3 months, which he never did.

hints.hx: Progressive fatigue and night sweats for 5 months. Cannot finish a normal-sized meal. No lymph node swelling. No fevers. No bleeding. Occasional left shoulder pain (referred pain from splenomegaly). Repeat WBC at PCP: 62,000 — referred urgently.
hints.pe: Splenomegaly: palpable 18cm below left costal margin — massive. Mild hepatomegaly. No peripheral lymphadenopathy. No sternal tenderness.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Massive splenomegaly — spleen 28cm longitudinal diameter. No significant lymphadenopathy. Liver 18cm.

lab_results.CBC: WBC 218,000 — differential shows full myeloid spectrum: neutrophils, bands, metamyelocytes, myelocytes, promyelocytes, and basophilia (8%). Hgb 9.6. Platelets 840,000.
lab_results.CMP: Uric acid 12.4. LDH 1,840. Vitamin B12 markedly elevated (>2000 pg/mL — released from granulocytes).
lab_results.Specialty: BCR-ABL1 quantitative PCR: Positive — ratio 82%. Cytogenetics: t(9;22)(q34;q11.2) — Philadelphia chromosome in 100% of metaphases. LAP score 8 (markedly low — differentiates from leukemoid reaction where LAP is high). Imatinib first-line.

hx_brief: 33yo female presents 6 weeks postpartum with worsening shortness of breath and bilateral leg swelling. She was discharged 6 weeks ago after an uncomplicated vaginal delivery and told to take it easy.

hints.hx: No cardiac history before or during pregnancy. Unable to lie flat since week 2 postpartum — attributed this to recovering from birth. Stopped breastfeeding last week because she was too short of breath. Lives alone and has been too exhausted to seek care sooner.
hints.pe: HR 116. BP 88/58. JVP elevated. Bilateral basal crackles. S3 gallop. Bilateral 3+ pitting edema to mid-thigh. Hepatojugular reflux positive.
hints.img: Echo: LV severely dilated (LVIDd 72mm). EF 22% — severely reduced. Moderate functional MR. No wall motion abnormalities. Cardiac MRI: Diffuse myocardial edema pattern without fibrosis — inflammatory process.

lab_results.EKG: Sinus tachycardia at 118. Diffuse low voltage. Occasional PVCs.
lab_results.Specialty: BNP 4,100 pg/mL. Prolactin level 88 ng/mL (elevated). Anti-cardiac antibody panel: Negative. Cardiac MRI: Diffuse subendocardial edema without fibrosis. Peripartum cardiomyopathy — occurs last month of pregnancy to 5 months postpartum.

hx_brief: 82yo male with BPH, hypertension, and mild cognitive impairment brought to the ED for confusion and decreased urine output over 2 days. He was recently started on oxybutynin for urinary urgency.

hints.hx: Aide reports much less urine over 2 days and he seems out of it. Fever since last night. Had a UTI treated 6 weeks ago. Oxybutynin started 3 weeks ago. Tamsulosin recently held due to orthostasis.
hints.pe: Temperature 39.2C. BP 84/52. HR 118. GCS 12. Suprapubic dullness to percussion — bladder palpable to umbilicus. Right CVA tenderness. Oxybutynin — anticholinergic — caused urinary retention in this BPH patient, creating obstruction above which infection developed.
hints.img: Renal Ultrasound: Bilateral hydronephrosis grade 3. Bladder markedly distended — 920mL estimated volume. Post-void residual 880mL after catheter placed.

lab_results.UA: Pyuria — WBCs too numerous to count. Positive nitrites. WBC casts present — infection localized to kidney.
lab_results.Specialty: Blood cultures x2: Gram-negative rods (preliminary). Urine culture: >100,000 CFU/mL E. coli — ESBL producing, carbapenem-sensitive. 30mL pus drained during ureteral stenting — obstructive pyelonephritis with urosepsis.

hx_brief: 47yo male is brought to the ED from a wedding reception in significant distress. He drank heavily over 18 hours and is now writhing in pain. His escort says 'he always gets belly pain when he drinks but it usually passes.'

hints.hx: Known hypertriglyceridemia, never treated — last checked 2 years ago at 980 mg/dL. Drinks heavily on weekends. This episode is different — pain is unrelenting, radiating straight through to his back, and vomiting has not brought relief. Leaning forward helps slightly.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.4C. HR 124. BP 96/68. Diaphoresis. Abdomen: diffuse guarding most severe in epigastrium. Hypoactive bowel sounds. Flank ecchymosis developing bilaterally (Grey Turner sign). Periumbilical ecchymosis (Cullen sign — retroperitoneal hemorrhage).
hints.img: CT Abdomen with contrast: Pancreatic parenchyma shows heterogeneous enhancement — areas of non-enhancement (necrosis). Extensive peripancreatic fat stranding and fluid in all three paracolic gutters. Moderately severe by Revised Atlanta Classification.

lab_results.Lipase: 8,400 U/L (>3x ULN). Serial lipase at 24hrs: 12,200 U/L.
lab_results.ABG: pO2 68 on room air — early ARDS. pH 7.32.
lab_results.Specialty: Triglycerides: 2,840 mg/dL (severely elevated — etiologic). LDH 680. Ranson criteria at 48hrs: 5 — high severity. Lipemic serum noted.

hx_brief: 42yo Black male presents for a second opinion after being treated for asthma for 14 months without improvement. His pulmonologist doubled his ICS dose twice and added a LABA, but his dyspnea worsens.

hints.hx: Non-smoker. Works as an HVAC technician. Spirometry shows restriction, not obstruction — inconsistent with asthma. Also mentions blurry vision in both eyes for 3 weeks and a red tender bump on his left shin.
hints.pe: Low-grade temperature 37.8C. Bilateral fine crackles. Anterior uveitis bilaterally on fundoscopy. Tender erythematous nodules on bilateral anterior shins (erythema nodosum). Bilateral hilum dullness on percussion. Parotid gland enlargement.
hints.img: CT Chest: Bilateral hilar and mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Perilymphatic micronodules on HRCT. PET: FDG uptake in lymph nodes, liver, spleen, and lacrimal glands.

lab_results.CMP: Calcium 11.8 (hypercalcemia — 1,25-OH-VitD produced by activated macrophages in granulomas). ACE level 88 U/L (elevated).
lab_results.EKG: Complete heart block (3rd degree) — sarcoid cardiac involvement.
lab_results.Specialty: BAL: CD4:CD8 ratio 5.2 (elevated; normal <3.5 — hallmark of sarcoidosis). Bronchoscopic biopsy: Non-caseating granulomas without central necrosis. AFB: Negative. Fungal culture: Negative. Complete heart block from sarcoid requires pacemaker.

hx_brief: 23yo male presents with 5 days of throat pain and now can barely open his mouth. He completed a 3-day course of azithromycin from leftover medication when symptoms started.

hints.hx: Throat pain started 10 days ago. Took azithromycin for 3 days from a prior prescription — felt slightly better, then dramatically worsened. Now drooling because he can't swallow his own saliva. Voice changed — sounds like he has a hot potato in his mouth. Trismus prevents adequate oral exam.
hints.pe: Temperature 39.4C. HR 108. Muffled, thick voice. Maximal trismus — mouth opens only 15mm (normal >35mm). Deviation of uvula to the right. Left peritonsillar fullness with medial displacement of the left tonsil. Drooling.
hints.img: CT Neck with contrast: 3.4cm rim-enhancing hypodense fluid collection in the left peritonsillar space. Mass effect on oropharynx with 40% airway narrowing. No parapharyngeal or retropharyngeal extension.

lab_results.Specialty: I&D drainage: 12mL frank pus. Culture: Group A Streptococcus pyogenes AND Fusobacterium nucleatum (oral anaerobe). Blood cultures: Negative. Rapid strep antigen: Positive. Note: Azithromycin resistance increasingly common in GAS — treatment failure contributed to abscess formation.

hx_brief: 41yo female presents for evaluation of hand swelling and morning stiffness that has been present for 11 months. She has been dismissed twice by urgent care as having 'stress-related joint pain.'

hints.hx: Bilateral hand and wrist swelling, worst in the morning — takes over 2 hours to loosen up. Fatigue is severe. Noticed a firm bump on her right elbow. No psoriasis. No photosensitivity. No Raynaud phenomenon. Mother has RA. Smokes half a pack daily.
hints.pe: Symmetric synovitis of bilateral MCPs, PIPs, and wrists — boggy, tender swelling. Early ulnar deviation of fingers. DIP joints spared. Firm non-tender nodule on right olecranon (rheumatoid nodule). No DIP or spine involvement.
hints.img: X-ray hands/wrists: Periarticular osteopenia bilateral MCPs and wrists. Early marginal erosions at 2nd and 3rd MCPs bilaterally. Ultrasound: Synovial hypertrophy with power Doppler signal — active synovitis.

lab_results.CBC: Hgb 10.4 — normocytic anemia of chronic inflammation. Thrombocytosis 540,000.
lab_results.Specialty: Anti-CCP: 340 U/mL (strongly positive — >3x ULN, high specificity for RA even before structural damage). RF: 240 IU/mL. CRP 52 mg/L. ESR 88mm/hr. ANA: Negative. Anti-dsDNA: Negative. DAS28 score 5.8 — high disease activity. Start methotrexate.

hx_brief: 48yo male with a known bicuspid aortic valve presents to cardiology follow-up for 'mild regurgitation found on echo 3 years ago.' His wife says he's been sleeping on 3 pillows and gets winded walking to the car.

hints.hx: Was told the regurgitation was mild and to 'just watch it.' Exertional dyspnea progressively worsening over 18 months — he stopped jogging 6 months ago. Now orthopneic. Occasional awareness of his heartbeat, especially lying on his left side. No chest pain. No syncope.
hints.pe: BP 158/54 — wide pulse pressure (104mmHg). HR 88. Bounding, water-hammer pulses at the radial arteries. Head bobs with each heartbeat (de Musset's sign). Blowing early diastolic decrescendo murmur at left sternal border, best in full expiration leaning forward. Displaced hyperdynamic apical impulse — laterally displaced PMI.
hints.img: Echo: Bicuspid aortic valve with severe aortic regurgitation. Regurgitant fraction 58%. LV severely dilated — LVIDd 72mm (normal <55mm). EF 42% (reduced — decompensating). Vena contracta 8mm. Pressure half-time 180ms (severe). Aortic root 4.6cm.

lab_results.EKG: LVH with strain pattern. Sinus tachycardia. Left axis deviation.
lab_results.ABG: pO2 74 on room air — mild hypoxemia.
lab_results.Specialty: Cath: LVEDP 28 mmHg (severely elevated — failing LV). Aortic regurgitant fraction confirmed 60%. No significant CAD. Surgical AVR indicated — LV dysfunction from chronic severe AR is partially reversible if corrected before EF drops below 25%.

hx_brief: 38yo male with a history of injection heroin use presents with 10 weeks of worsening lower back pain and bilateral leg weakness. He was seen at the ED twice and discharged with muscle relaxants.

hints.hx: Active IV drug use. Lower back pain constant, worse at night, not relieved by any position — pathological pattern. Two weeks ago noticed bilateral thigh weakness and trouble going up stairs. Had dental work 4 months ago. Fevers and drenching night sweats for 8 weeks.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.9C. Exquisite point tenderness over L3-L4 vertebrae. Lower extremity weakness 3/5 bilaterally. Hyperreflexia with upgoing Babinski bilaterally — upper motor neuron signs. Perianal sensation reduced. Rectal tone decreased.
hints.img: MRI Spine with gadolinium: Rim-enhancing epidural abscess L3-L4 with cord compression and myelopathy signal. Vertebral endplate erosion and disc space narrowing L3-L4 — discitis/osteomyelitis. Contiguous epidural extension.

lab_results.CBC: WBC 24,200 with left shift. ESR 118. CRP 184.
lab_results.Specialty: Blood cultures x2 (drawn before antibiotics): MSSA. TEE: 1.4cm vegetation on tricuspid valve — concurrent infective endocarditis. Disc aspiration culture: MSSA. Source: IV drug use → bacteremia → seeding via Batson plexus → vertebral osteomyelitis → epidural abscess.

hx_brief: 17yo female is brought to the ED by her parents after fainting at school. She denies anything is wrong. She exercises 4 hours daily and has been restricting her diet for 8 months 'to be healthier.'

hints.hx: Lost 32 pounds over 8 months. Periods stopped 5 months ago — parents unaware. Perceives herself as overweight despite being visibly underweight. Feels cold constantly. Fine hair growing on her arms. Mood flat. Denies bingeing, purging, or laxative use.
hints.pe: BMI 13.8. HR 36. BP 74/46. Temperature 35.8C (hypothermic). Lanugo on arms, back, and face. Bilateral parotid enlargement. Peripheral edema. Oral exam: Dental enamel intact (no purging). Yellow-orange skin (hypercarotenemia).
hints.img: Echo: Moderate pericardial effusion. EF 58% (preserved but low-normal). Mitral valve prolapse — common in low body weight.

lab_results.CBC: Pancytopenia — WBC 2.8, Hgb 9.2, Platelets 88,000 — hypocellular marrow from starvation.
lab_results.CMP: Na 130. K 2.8 (dangerous hypokalemia). Glucose 44 (hypoglycemia). Phosphorus 1.4 (low). Albumin 2.4. Cholesterol paradoxically elevated 288.
lab_results.EKG: Sinus bradycardia at 34 bpm. Prolonged QTc 498ms — risk of fatal arrhythmia.
lab_results.Specialty: DEXA: T-score -3.1 lumbar spine — severe osteoporosis. FSH 1.2, LH 0.8 (suppressed). Estradiol <10 pg/mL (undetectable). IGF-1 markedly low. Classic hypothalamic suppression from severe caloric restriction.

hx_brief: 71yo male referred by his neurologist for progressive confusion and urinary incontinence over 5 months. The family is devastated — he was a sharp retired engineer. Alzheimer workup has been negative.

hints.hx: Gait changed first — started shuffling and taking very small steps 6 months ago. Cognitive decline followed. Now incontinent of urine, which he finds profoundly embarrassing. Had a subarachnoid hemorrhage 7 months ago managed with coiling. No family history of dementia.
hints.pe: Alert but disoriented to date and place. Broad-based shuffling magnetic gait — feet appear glued to the floor. MMSE 19/30 — impaired executive function and recall. Grasp reflex present. No Parkinsonian features. No focal deficits.
hints.img: MRI Brain: Markedly dilated lateral and third ventricles — out of proportion to sulcal atrophy. Periventricular T2/FLAIR hyperintensity — transependymal CSF flow. No cortical atrophy. The triad (gait→cognition→incontinence) in sequence after SAH is classic.

lab_results.Specialty: LP: Opening pressure 21 cmH2O (upper normal). CSF: Normal protein, glucose, cells. Large volume tap: Removal of 40mL CSF. Gait assessment 1-hour post-tap: 10-meter walk test improved from 28 to 16 seconds. MMSE improved 4 points. Predicts excellent response to VP shunting.

hx_brief: 48yo female gardener presents with 4 months of proximal muscle weakness and a violaceous discoloration around her eyes that worsened after spending long days outdoors.

hints.hx: First noticed difficulty climbing out of her truck and raising her arms above her head to prune trees. Eye discoloration initially attributed to sun exposure. Rough cracked skin on hands — hands are her livelihood as a gardener. New dry cough for 3 weeks.
hints.pe: Heliotrope discoloration of eyelids bilaterally — purple-red. V-sign erythema on anterior chest. Gottron papules over MCP and PIP knuckles. Shawl sign on posterior shoulders. Proximal upper and lower extremity strength 2/5. Mechanic's hands — hyperkeratotic cracked palmar skin.
hints.img: MRI Thighs: Diffuse T2 signal and enhancement in proximal muscle groups bilaterally — active myositis. CT Chest: Ground-glass opacities bilateral lower lobes — organizing pneumonia pattern (ILD).

lab_results.UA: Myoglobinuria — urine dipstick positive for blood with no RBCs on microscopy.
lab_results.ABG: pO2 72 on room air — hypoxemia from ILD.
lab_results.Specialty: CK: 12,800 U/L (markedly elevated). Anti-Jo-1: Positive (antisynthetase syndrome). ANA 1:320 speckled. EMG: Short-duration, low-amplitude polyphasic potentials — myopathic. CT Chest and abdomen: No occult malignancy. Dermatomyositis in patients over 45 requires malignancy screening.

hx_brief: 59yo male smoker presents to the ED via EMS after his wife found him diaphoretic and pale on the couch at 6am. He says the chest pain started at 4am but he 'didn't want to bother anyone.'

hints.hx: Crushing substernal pressure radiating to his left jaw for 2 hours. Diaphoresis and nausea. Denies shortness of breath. Hypertensive, last A1c 8.8%, never saw a cardiologist. Brother had CABG at 54.
hints.pe: BP 86/54 (cardiogenic shock). HR 112. Diaphoretic and pale. Lungs clear (anterior MI — no backward failure yet). S4 gallop. JVP not elevated. Peripheral pulses diminished. Extremities cool and mottled.
hints.img: Stat bedside Echo: Anterior wall and apical hypokinesis. EF approximately 25-30%. Urgent coronary angiography: 100% thrombotic occlusion proximal LAD — TIMI 0 flow. Thrombus burden high.

lab_results.EKG: ST elevation 4-6mm V1-V5. Reciprocal ST depression II, III, aVF. De Winter T-waves in V3-V4 (proximal LAD occlusion pattern).
lab_results.Trop: Initial Troponin I 24 ng/mL — rising. 3-hour: 86 ng/mL. 6-hour: 148 ng/mL (peak).
lab_results.Specialty: Post-PCI: TIMI 3 flow restored with drug-eluting stent in proximal LAD. IABP placed for cardiogenic shock. Post-procedure EF 28%. Late presentation (2 hours) contributed to large infarct and shock.

hx_brief: 31yo male presents with 3 months of bloody diarrhea that his PCP has been treating as infectious colitis. Three stool cultures have been negative.

hints.hx: 8-15 bloody mucoid stools daily. Lost 14 pounds. Night sweats. Cannot leave the house due to urgency and fecal incontinence. Bilateral knee and ankle pain started 3 weeks ago. New redness in his right eye. NSAIDs prescribed for joint pain worsened his diarrhea significantly.
hints.pe: Pale. HR 108. Diffuse lower abdominal tenderness worst in the LLQ. No rebound or peritoneal signs. Right anterior uveitis. Bilateral ankle effusions. Oral aphthous ulcers.
hints.img: CT Abdomen/Pelvis: Continuous colonic wall thickening from rectum extending to splenic flexure. Mucosal enhancement. No skip lesions. No terminal ileal involvement. Colonoscopy: Erythema, friability, and spontaneous bleeding from rectum to splenic flexure — sharp transition to normal. No rectal sparing.

lab_results.CBC: Hgb 8.4 — normocytic anemia. WBC 16,400. Platelets 680,000 (reactive thrombocytosis).
lab_results.CMP: Albumin 2.2 (severely low — protein-losing enteropathy). CRP 92 mg/L. ESR 114mm/hr.
lab_results.Specialty: Biopsies: Crypt architectural distortion. Crypt abscesses — neutrophilic cryptitis. Basal plasmacytosis. No granulomas. No dysplasia. p-ANCA: Positive. ASCA: Negative. Fecal calprotectin: 1,840 mcg/g. C. diff: Negative x3. Mayo endoscopic score 3 (severe).

hx_brief: 22yo male presents with a swollen right knee for 5 days. He was seen at urgent care and told he sprained it at the gym, but he doesn't recall any injury.

hints.hx: No trauma. Woke up 5 days ago with a hot swollen knee. Two small tender papular skin lesions on his right palm — dismissed as insect bites. Sexually active with multiple partners, last encounter 4 weeks ago. Low-grade fever since yesterday. Wrist and ankle pain that came and went before the knee swelling.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.2C. Right knee: markedly warm, swollen, painful, limited ROM in all directions. 2 tender pustular lesions on right palm and sole. Bilateral wrist tenderness without swelling (tenosynovitis). Left ankle effusion.
hints.img: X-ray right knee: Large joint effusion, soft tissue swelling — no bony erosions. MRI right knee: Joint effusion with synovial thickening and enhancement.

lab_results.Specialty: Joint aspiration right knee: 42mL turbid fluid. WBC 82,000/uL (95% PMNs — septic range). Gram stain: Gram-negative diplococci (intracellular). Culture: Neisseria gonorrhoeae. Urine NAAT: Gonorrhea positive. Throat and rectal NAAT: Both positive. Migratory arthritis + tenosynovitis + pustular rash = DGI triad.

hx_brief: 68yo male retired professor presents after his wife insisted. She says he has become 'robotic' over 2 years — moving slowly, rarely expressing emotion, and his handwriting has become unreadably small.

hints.hx: Right hand trembles when he rests it on his desk — stops when he reaches for something. Difficulty initiating walking — feet feel glued to the floor. Soft voice. Has fallen twice in the past 3 months. No dementia. No hallucinations yet.
hints.pe: Masked facies — hypomimia. Monotone hypophonia. Cogwheel rigidity bilateral upper extremities. Resting pill-rolling tremor right hand (4-6Hz). Bradykinesia — slowed rapid alternating movements. Postural instability — pull test positive (2 steps backward). Gait: shuffling, reduced arm swing, en-bloc turning.
hints.img: MRI Brain: No structural lesion. No vascular disease. DaTscan SPECT: Asymmetric reduced dopamine transporter uptake — right putamen > left putamen. Comma-shaped uptake pattern lost.

lab_results.Specialty: DaTscan SPECT: Asymmetric loss of dopaminergic terminal density bilateral striata — right more than left (contralateral to symptomatic side). Rules out drug-induced parkinsonism and essential tremor (both have normal DaTscan). Alpha-synuclein seed amplification assay: Positive — supports Lewy body pathology.

hx_brief: 27yo male referred for recurrent jaundice. He has been worked up extensively twice and told his liver is fine. Now presenting again after three days of fasting for a colonoscopy prep.

hints.hx: Jaundice appears predictably during illness, fasting, stress, or after intense exercise and resolves spontaneously within 48 hours. First episode at age 19. Normal liver enzymes on multiple occasions. No alcohol use. No medications. Feels completely well during jaundice episodes.
hints.pe: Mild scleral icterus — the only abnormal finding. No hepatomegaly. No splenomegaly. No spider angiomas. No ascites. No Kayser-Fleischer rings. Neurologically intact.
hints.img: RUQ Ultrasound: Normal liver echogenicity, size, and texture. No biliary dilation. No gallstones.

lab_results.CBC: Normal. No hemolysis — normal reticulocyte count, normal haptoglobin, negative Coombs.
lab_results.CMP: Total bilirubin 4.8 mg/dL — predominantly unconjugated (indirect 4.4). Direct bilirubin 0.4 (normal). ALT, AST, ALP, GGT all normal. Albumin normal. PT normal.
lab_results.UA: No bilirubin in urine (conjugated bilirubin would appear in urine — its absence confirms unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia).
lab_results.Specialty: UGT1A1 genotyping: Homozygous (TA)7/(TA)7 repeat in promoter region (7/7 genotype). Gilbert syndrome confirmed — benign, no treatment required. Reduced UGT1A1 activity → impaired conjugation → indirect hyperbilirubinemia precipitated by fasting.

hx_brief: 52yo male with a 20-year history of heavy alcohol use presents with progressive exertional dyspnea and bilateral leg swelling over 6 weeks. He stopped drinking 8 months ago after a hospitalization.

hints.hx: Drinks approximately 500mL vodka daily for 20 years until 8 months ago. No prior cardiac history. Cannot lie flat — sleeping in a recliner chair. Bilateral ankle swelling worsening. Intermittent palpitations. His primary care attributed his dyspnea to deconditioning.
hints.pe: HR 124 irregularly irregular. BP 88/60. JVP 16cm. Bilateral crackles halfway up lung fields. S3 at apex. 4+ pitting edema to mid-thigh. Hepatomegaly 4cm. Ascites — shifting dullness present.
hints.img: Echo: All four chambers dilated. EF 14% (severely reduced). No wall motion abnormalities — global hypokinesis. Moderate-to-severe functional MR. Moderate-to-severe TR.

lab_results.CBC: MCV 112 (macrocytosis — thiamine and folate deficiency). Hgb 9.2.
lab_results.CMP: Na 126 (dilutional). GGT 440 (markedly elevated). AST:ALT ratio 2.4 (>2:1 — alcoholic pattern). Albumin 2.6.
lab_results.EKG: Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response at 136bpm.
lab_results.Specialty: BNP 6,200 pg/mL. Thiamine level: Undetectable. Selenium level: Low. EtOH level: 0 (confirmed abstinent). Alcoholic dilated cardiomyopathy — abstinence can lead to partial EF recovery.

hx_brief: 26yo highly myopic female presents after suddenly losing vision in her left eye while reading. She describes it as a dark curtain falling from above.

hints.hx: Myopia -11 diopters bilaterally — extreme myopia is the strongest risk factor. Two days before vision loss she noticed new floaters in the left eye and brief flashes of light in the periphery — photopsia from vitreoretinal traction. Dismissed these as benign. No trauma.
hints.pe: Left eye visual acuity: counting fingers at 2 feet. Right eye: 20/25. Left fundoscopy: Billowing gray elevation of retina with loss of underlying choroidal detail — detached retina. Right eye: Peripheral lattice degeneration — at-risk lesion.
hints.img: Ocular ultrasound B-scan: Highly reflective membrane detached from posterior pole, anchored at optic disc. Horseshoe tear at 11 o'clock meridian — identified as etiology.

lab_results.Specialty: Fluorescein angiography: Large superotemporal rhegmatogenous retinal detachment involving the macula. Horseshoe tear at 11 o'clock with subretinal fluid extending to posterior pole. Macular involvement confirmed — prognosis for vision recovery reduced after 72 hours of macular-off detachment.

hx_brief: 52yo male of Irish descent presents with fatigue, joint pain in his knuckles, and his wife saying he looks 'bronze.' His PCP initially attributed it to working outdoors.

hints.hx: Bronze skin discoloration progressively worsening over 3 years — most pronounced on sun-exposed areas and knuckles. Severe fatigue. Arthropathy of the 2nd and 3rd MCPs — firm, tender swelling. Loss of libido and erectile dysfunction. No alcohol use. Father had 'liver disease' of unclear cause and died at 58.
hints.pe: Bronze/slate-gray skin pigmentation. Hepatomegaly — liver edge 5cm below costal margin, firm. MCP joints bilaterally enlarged with hook-like osteophytes palpable. Testicular atrophy. No spider angiomata. No asterixis yet.
hints.img: MRI Liver: Diffuse T2 hypointensity throughout the hepatic parenchyma — iron deposition pattern. Liver biopsy: Prussian blue staining confirms massive iron deposition in hepatocytes and Kupffer cells. Hepatic iron index >2. No cirrhosis yet — periportal fibrosis only.

lab_results.CBC: Hgb 11.4 — mild normocytic anemia (late finding from liver disease).
lab_results.CMP: AST 88, ALT 72 (elevated). Glucose 198 — new onset diabetes (bronze diabetes from pancreatic iron deposition). Albumin 3.2.
lab_results.Specialty: Serum ferritin: 4,800 ng/mL (markedly elevated; normal <300). Transferrin saturation: 88% (normal <45%). HFE gene: Homozygous C282Y mutation — classic hereditary hemochromatosis. Echocardiogram: Restrictive cardiomyopathy from myocardial iron deposition.

hx_brief: 38yo female presents to the ED 4 days after returning from a 16-hour flight from Tokyo. She has sudden onset dyspnea and right-sided pleuritic chest pain that woke her from sleep.

hints.hx: Window seat — moved minimally during the flight. On combined oral contraceptive pill for 8 years. No prior clots. Left leg swollen and tender since landing, attributed to 'flight swelling.' O2 sat 87% on room air. Mother had a DVT at 42 (factor V Leiden found).
hints.pe: HR 122. BP 96/68. O2 sat 87% on 15L non-rebreather. JVP elevated. Left calf erythematous and tender.
hints.img: CT Pulmonary Angiogram: Saddle embolus at the main pulmonary artery bifurcation extending into bilateral main pulmonary arteries. Right heart strain. Bedside echo: RV dilation, D-sign, McConnell sign, TAPSE reduced.

lab_results.EKG: Sinus tachycardia at 126. S1Q3T3 pattern. New RBBB. T-wave inversions V1-V4.
lab_results.Trop: Troponin I 2.8 ng/mL (RV strain — submassive PE with hemodynamic instability).
lab_results.ABG: pH 7.50. pO2 52. pCO2 26. A-a gradient 72 — severe V/Q mismatch.
lab_results.Specialty: Echo: RV:LV ratio 1.4. McConnell sign confirmed. RVSP 66mmHg. Factor V Leiden heterozygous. OCP + Factor V Leiden + immobility = Virchow's triad. Submassive (intermediate-high risk) PE — systemic thrombolytics considered.

hx_brief: 14yo male presents with tea-colored urine and facial puffiness for 4 days. His parents treated him for allergies initially. He had a sore throat 3 weeks ago.

hints.hx: Strep throat 3 weeks ago — treated with 5 days of amoxicillin (not the full 10 days). Puffiness worse in the morning around his eyes. Tea-colored urine started 4 days after completing antibiotics. He gained 4 pounds in 1 week despite eating the same.
hints.pe: BP 158/96 (hypertension for age). Bilateral periorbital edema most pronounced in the morning. Bilateral 1+ pitting edema lower extremities. Temperature 37.1C.
hints.img: Renal ultrasound: Bilaterally enlarged and echogenic kidneys. No hydronephrosis. No stones.

lab_results.CMP: Creatinine 2.4 (elevated for age). BUN 48. Na 132. Phosphorus mildly elevated.
lab_results.UA: Cola-colored. 4+ blood. 2+ protein. RBC casts on microscopy — pathognomonic of glomerulonephritis. Dysmorphic RBCs. Urine protein:creatinine 3.2.
lab_results.Specialty: ASO titer: 1:1280 (markedly elevated). Anti-DNase B: Positive. C3: 28 mg/dL (markedly low; normal 90-180). C4: Normal — C3 consumption via alternative pathway is classic for PSGN. ANA: Negative. ANCA: Negative.

hx_brief: 68yo male presents with a 3-month history of regurgitating undigested food, progressive difficulty swallowing, and a gurgling sound in his neck after eating. He has lost 16 pounds.

hints.hx: Food regurgitated hours after eating — undigested and odorless (no stomach acid). Presses on his left neck to help food go down — relieves symptoms temporarily. Woke up twice choking on regurgitated food. No heartburn ever. Recent aspiration pneumonia.
hints.pe: Mild halitosis despite good dental hygiene. Soft gurgling swelling in the left posterior neck — Boyce sign (gurgling on compression). No lymphadenopathy. Weight 62kg (was 78kg).
hints.img: Barium swallow: Posterior outpouching at the pharyngoesophageal junction — Killian's triangle. Contrast pooling in diverticulum before entering esophagus. Endoscopy: Blind-ended pouch just above the cricopharyngeal muscle — DO NOT scope aggressively (perforation risk).

lab_results.Specialty: Manometry: Incomplete and incoordinated cricopharyngeal relaxation. Upper esophageal sphincter pressure elevated at 120mmHg (normal 40-80mmHg). Zenker diverticulum confirmed — posterior herniation through Killian's dehiscence due to cricopharyngeal dysfunction.

hx_brief: 29yo male admitted for pneumonia 8 days ago, treated with clindamycin and discharged. Now returning with 15 watery stools per day, severe abdominal cramping, and a fever.

hints.hx: Completed the full clindamycin course for aspiration pneumonia. Diarrhea started on day 6 of antibiotics — initially watery, now with mucus and traces of blood. Lower abdominal cramping waking him from sleep.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.8C. HR 114. BP 102/68. Diffuse abdominal tenderness without peritoneal signs. Abdomen distended. Decreased bowel sounds.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Pancolonic wall thickening from cecum to rectum. Pericolonic fat stranding. Thumbprinting pattern throughout. No pneumatosis. No free air. Colon 7cm at widest — approaching toxic megacolon.

lab_results.CBC: WBC 32,400 (markedly elevated — leukocytosis this severe is a red flag for fulminant C. diff). Bands 22%.
lab_results.Specialty: C. difficile toxin B PCR: Positive. GDH antigen: Positive. Toxin A/B EIA: Positive. Stool culture: C. difficile ribotype 027 (NAP1 strain) — hypervirulent, fluoroquinolone-resistant, produces binary toxin. Severity classification: Severe-complicated — requires oral vancomycin.

hx_brief: 54yo female presents with a 9-month history of intractable itching, jaundice, and fatigue. She has seen three dermatologists for the itching — all without success.

hints.hx: Itching preceded jaundice by 4 months and is worst at night — not relieved by antihistamines or topical steroids. Dark urine and pale stools for 3 months. Dry eyes requiring artificial tears. Dry mouth — can't eat crackers without water. No alcohol, no hepatitis exposures.
hints.pe: Moderate scleral icterus. Xanthelasma bilaterally. Xanthomas on extensor surfaces. Excoriations on trunk from chronic scratching. Hepatomegaly 3cm below costal margin. No spider angiomas.
hints.img: MRCP: No biliary obstruction. Intrahepatic bile ducts normal caliber. No gallstones. No mass. Normal pancreatic duct.

lab_results.CMP: ALP 880 (markedly elevated — cholestatic pattern). GGT 640. Total bilirubin 6.2. Direct 4.8. AST 68, ALT 72 (mildly elevated). Albumin 3.0. Total IgM markedly elevated.
lab_results.Specialty: AMA: Positive M2 titer 1:2560 (>95% specific for PBC). Anti-sp100: Positive. Anti-gp210: Positive. Liver biopsy: Florid bile duct lesion — granulomatous destruction of medium-sized intrahepatic bile ducts. Ludwig Stage III.

hx_brief: 48yo male with known lumbar disc disease presents to the ED after waking from sleep with severe bilateral leg pain and the inability to urinate. He assumed it was a disc flare.

hints.hx: Lumbar disc herniations at L4-L5 and L5-S1 documented 2 years ago — managed conservatively. This pain is fundamentally different — bilateral, severe, started abruptly. Bladder has been feeling full since yesterday but cannot void. Perianal area feels numb.
hints.pe: Lower extremity weakness 3/5 bilaterally. Reduced perianal sensation to light touch and pinprick — saddle anesthesia. Reduced anal sphincter tone on rectal exam — finger barely resisted. Bladder dullness to percussion to the umbilicus. Bilateral Achilles reflexes absent.
hints.img: MRI Lumbar Spine STAT: Large central L4-L5 disc herniation with severe compression of the entire cauda equina nerve root complex. T2 signal within compressed roots — early ischemic change.

lab_results.Specialty: Post-void residual (after urethral catheter): 780mL — urinary retention. Urodynamics: Neurogenic bladder pattern — acontractile detrusor. Absent H-reflexes. Time-sensitive emergency: Best outcomes with surgical decompression within 24-48 hours of symptom onset.

hx_brief: 19yo female presents with 6 days of lower abdominal pain, fever, and vaginal discharge. She was seen at student health 3 days ago and told she had a UTI — antibiotics not helping.

hints.hx: New partner 4 weeks ago. Inconsistent condom use. Urinalysis done at student health was normal — the UTI diagnosis was incorrect. Bilateral lower abdominal pain worsening with movement. Purulent vaginal discharge started 2 days ago.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.3C. HR 104. Bilateral lower quadrant tenderness — right worse than left. Pelvic exam: Mucopurulent endocervical discharge. Cervical motion tenderness — strongly positive (chandelier sign). Bilateral adnexal tenderness and fullness.
hints.img: Pelvic Ultrasound: Bilateral thickened fluid-filled fallopian tubes (tubo-ovarian complex). Small amount of free fluid in pelvis. MRI Pelvis: Bilateral salpingitis with peritoneal inflammatory changes.

lab_results.UA: Normal — no pyuria, no nitrites. This proves the initial UTI diagnosis was incorrect.
lab_results.Specialty: Cervical NAAT: Chlamydia trachomatis POSITIVE. Neisseria gonorrhoeae: Negative. Rectal NAAT Chlamydia: Positive. HIV: Negative. Treatment: Ceftriaxone 500mg IM x1 + Doxycycline 100mg BID x14 days + Metronidazole 500mg BID x14 days.

hx_brief: 71yo male with a known bicuspid aortic valve presents with exertional dyspnea, two syncopal episodes during walks, and progressive angina over 8 months. He has been avoiding activities to prevent symptoms.

hints.hx: Bicuspid aortic valve diagnosed incidentally on echo 22 years ago — never followed up. Syncope occurred specifically during exertion — classic for critical AS. Exercise tolerance dropped from 2 miles to 50 feet. Chest tightness with any activity. SAD triad.
hints.pe: BP 104/82 — narrow pulse pressure (22mmHg). Slow-rising carotid upstroke (pulsus parvus et tardus). Harsh crescendo-decrescendo systolic murmur grade 4/6 at RUSB radiating to bilateral carotids. Diminished and paradoxically split S2. Sustained apex beat (pressure overload).
hints.img: Echo: Bicuspid aortic valve severely calcified and restricted. Peak gradient 92mmHg. Mean gradient 58mmHg. AVA 0.6 cm2 (severe: <1.0cm2). EF 42% (newly reduced from 60% last year — decompensating).

lab_results.EKG: LVH with strain pattern. Left axis deviation. QRS duration 118ms.
lab_results.Specialty: Right heart cath: PCWP 22mmHg (elevated). CO 3.2 L/min (reduced — low-flow state). AVA confirmed 0.6cm2 by Gorlin formula. Very high surgical risk — TAVR recommended over SAVR given age. The 3 symptoms (syncope, angina, dyspnea) each carry prognostic weight — survival without surgery is 1-3 years.

hx_brief: 28yo male referred for evaluation of 7 weeks of daily high spiking fevers and joint pain. He has been worked up for infectious endocarditis, lymphoma, and adult-onset lupus — all negative.

hints.hx: Fevers spike to 39.5-40.5C daily in the late afternoon and normalize by morning — quotidian pattern. Rash appears only during fever spikes and vanishes completely when afebrile — evanescent. Multiple joints symmetrically involved. All autoimmune tests negative. Lost 12 pounds.
hints.pe: During fever spike: Salmon-colored evanescent macular rash on trunk — fades with pressure, disappears within 30 minutes of defervescing. Splenomegaly 4cm. Lymphadenopathy in cervical and axillary regions. Symmetric synovitis of bilateral wrists, MCPs, knees, and ankles.
hints.img: CT Chest/Abdomen: Mediastinal and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. Splenomegaly 16cm. No masses. PET: Diffuse FDG uptake in lymph nodes, spleen, and synovium.

lab_results.Specialty: Ferritin: 68,000 ng/mL (markedly elevated — >3,000 is highly suggestive of AOSD; >10,000 is near-diagnostic). Glycosylated ferritin fraction: 20% (normal >50% — low glycosylated ferritin is characteristic of AOSD). RF: Negative. ANA: Negative. All cultures and serology: Negative. Yamaguchi criteria met.

hx_brief: 56yo male presents at 2am with sudden onset excruciating right great toe pain that woke him from sleep. He cannot tolerate the weight of his bedsheet.

hints.hx: Three prior episodes of severe podagra over 5 years — always treated with ibuprofen and resolved. Recently started hydrochlorothiazide for hypertension. Drinks 5-6 beers nightly and eats shellfish weekly. Never started urate-lowering therapy despite prior attacks.
hints.pe: Right first MTP joint: erythematous, warm, markedly swollen — appears almost infected. Exquisitely tender to even light touch. ROM severely limited. Tophi palpable on right ear helix and bilateral Achilles tendons.
hints.img: X-ray right foot: Periarticular erosions with overhanging edges and sclerotic margins at first MTP — rat-bite erosions. Soft tissue density deposits (tophi) visible adjacent to joint. No calcification (distinguishes from CPPD).

lab_results.CMP: Creatinine 1.6 (mild CKD — impaired urate excretion). Uric acid 12.8 mg/dL.
lab_results.Specialty: Joint aspiration: 4mL turbid fluid. WBC 48,000/uL (85% PMNs). Polarized microscopy: Needle-shaped negatively birefringent monosodium urate crystals (yellow when parallel to compensator axis — key distinction from CPPD which is weakly positively birefringent). No organisms on Gram stain.

hx_brief: 31yo female presents with 4 months of progressive proximal muscle weakness and a rash on her knuckles that she treated with hydrocortisone cream without improvement.

hints.hx: Works as a hiking guide. Difficulty rising from squat or climbing stairs. Worsening over months. Dry nonproductive cough started 3 weeks ago. No joint swelling. No prior autoimmune history. Rash specifically on knuckles — not palms or dorsum of hand.
hints.pe: Scaly erythematous papules over MCP and PIP joints bilaterally (Gottron papules). V-sign erythema on anterior chest. Proximal upper and lower extremity strength 2+/5. Mechanic's hands. No heliotrope rash. Fine bibasilar crackles on lung auscultation.
hints.img: MRI Thighs: Diffuse T2 signal hyperintensity and enhancement proximal muscle groups bilaterally — active myositis pattern.

lab_results.CMP: AST 320, ALT 268 (markedly elevated — muscle enzyme leak). LDH 680.
lab_results.UA: Dipstick blood 2+ — no RBCs on microscopy. Myoglobinuria.
lab_results.ABG: pO2 68 on room air — ILD causing hypoxemia.
lab_results.Specialty: CK: 14,200 U/L. Anti-MDA5: Positive (MDA5 = melanoma differentiation-associated gene 5). Anti-Jo-1: Negative. HRCT Chest: Bilateral ground-glass opacities and consolidation — organizing pneumonia pattern with rapidly progressive ILD (anti-MDA5 associated — high mortality risk). CT Abdomen: No malignancy.

hx_brief: 47yo male who has had 6 peptic ulcers in 3 years presents for management. Each ulcer healed on PPIs but recurred immediately on discontinuation. His GI doctor suspects H. pylori but all three tests have been negative.
