hints.hx: Ulcers in unusual locations — distal duodenum and proximal jejunum (not typical H. pylori distribution). Chronic watery diarrhea for 2 years — 6-8 stools per day regardless of diet. Lost 18 pounds from malabsorption. Father had a pancreatic tumor. Steatorrhea — fatty floating stools.
hints.pe: Mild epigastric tenderness without guarding. No palpable abdominal mass. Mild hepatomegaly.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Prominent gastric mucosal folds. Thickening of duodenal and proximal jejunal walls. Subtle 1.4cm hypodense lesion in pancreatic head. OctreoScan: Uptake in pancreatic head lesion.

lab_results.Lipase: 580 U/L — mildly elevated from acid damage to pancreatic ducts.
lab_results.Specialty: Fasting serum gastrin: 2,200 pg/mL (markedly elevated; normal <100pg/mL). Secretin stimulation test: Paradoxical rise in gastrin >200 pg/mL — pathognomonic for gastrinoma. BAO: 28 mEq/hr (markedly elevated). MEN1 gene mutation NEGATIVE (sporadic gastrinoma). PPI held 1 week before gastrin testing.

hx_brief: 32yo male presents with sudden onset tearing chest pain radiating to his back that woke him at 3am. He is 6'7" and was told he had a 'heart murmur' as a teenager that was never investigated.

hints.hx: Father died of an aortic emergency at age 41. History of spontaneous lens dislocation (ectopia lentis) at age 14. Joint hypermobility since childhood. Arm span greater than height. Pain is maximal at onset — tearing, migratory from front to back.
hints.pe: Height 6'7". Arm span 82 inches (exceeds height). Arachnodactyly. High-arched palate. Pectus excavatum. Aortic regurgitation murmur (early diastolic blowing at left sternal border). BP: Right arm 188/56, Left arm 162/60 (differential suggesting dissection).
hints.img: CTA Chest: Type A aortic dissection — intimal flap from aortic root to descending aorta. Aortic root diameter 7.2cm (severely dilated). Pericardial effusion — hemopericardium.

lab_results.Trop: 0.14 ng/mL (mildly elevated — aortic root involvement compromising RCA).
lab_results.Specialty: FBN1 sequencing: Pathogenic missense variant c.5788G>A — confirmed Marfan syndrome. Echo: Aortic root 7.2cm. Mitral valve prolapse. Pericardial effusion 1.4cm. Ophthalmology: Bilateral superior ectopia lentis. Father's death now explained.

hx_brief: 61yo male with 30 years of shipbuilding work presents with progressive dyspnea and right-sided chest heaviness over 4 months. His PCP initially attributed it to COPD despite him never smoking.

hints.hx: Worked in shipyard insulating steam pipes with asbestos-containing materials from age 20 to 50. Never smoked. Progressive breathlessness — now at rest. Right chest heaviness. Night sweats and 15-pound weight loss. Seen 30 years after exposure — classic latency period for mesothelioma (20-50 years).
hints.pe: Reduced expansion right hemithorax. Dullness to percussion right base extending to mid-lung. Absent breath sounds right lower lobe. No clubbing. Mild cervical lymphadenopathy.
hints.img: CT Chest: Large right pleural effusion with underlying pleural rind and nodularity. Pleural thickening encasing the right lung. Calcified pleural plaques bilaterally (asbestos exposure marker). PET: FDG-avid pleural thickening.

lab_results.Specialty: Thoracentesis: 1.4L exudative fluid. LDH 680 (markedly elevated). Cytology: Malignant mesothelial cells. CT-guided pleural biopsy: Epithelioid mesothelioma — calretinin positive, WT-1 positive, CK5/6 positive, CEA negative, TTF-1 negative. BAP1 mutation: Positive.

hx_brief: 21yo male college student is brought to the ED by his roommate for bizarre behavior over 4 days. The roommate says he's a different person — alternately aggressive, confused, and catatonic.

hints.hx: No psychiatric history. Toxicology negative. Had a URI with low-grade fever 3 weeks ago. Parents report a seizure at home 2 days ago. Now hypersexual, hallucinating, and making repetitive mouth movements. Autonomic instability — fever and blood pressure fluctuations.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.4C. GCS 12 — fluctuating. Orofacial dyskinesias — repetitive lip smacking and tongue protrusion. Confused, agitated. Autonomic instability: labile BP and HR. No focal deficits. No rash. No lymphadenopathy.
hints.img: MRI Brain FLAIR: Bilateral medial temporal lobe T2 signal hyperintensity. Hippocampal edema. No mass effect. EEG: Diffuse slow-wave activity, no ictal activity.

lab_results.Specialty: CSF: Lymphocytic pleocytosis (42 cells/uL). Protein mildly elevated 58mg/dL. Anti-NMDA receptor antibody (GluN1 subunit): Positive in CSF at 1:40. Positive in serum at 1:320. CT Abdomen: No teratoma (ovarian teratoma in ~50% of female cases — male cases less commonly have tumor). Most common autoimmune encephalitis.

hx_brief: 52yo male presents to his PCP with 8 weeks of cough, low-grade fevers, and night sweats. He is immunocompetent and his chest X-ray shows bilateral infiltrates. Three courses of antibiotics haven't helped.

hints.hx: Lived in Indiana near the Ohio River for 25 years. Works in construction — demolition and soil excavation. Moved to Arizona 6 months ago. Illness started 4 weeks after a large demolition project. Non-smoker. Not on immunosuppression.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.1C. Bilateral fine crackles. Mild hepatomegaly. Cervical lymphadenopathy. Oral exam: shallow painless ulcers on buccal mucosa.
hints.img: CT Chest: Bilateral nodular infiltrates. Mediastinal and hilar lymphadenopathy. CT Abdomen: Hepatomegaly with multiple hypodense lesions — disseminated disease.

lab_results.Specialty: Urine Histoplasma antigen: Positive (high titer 8.4 ng/mL — consistent with disseminated disease). Serum antigen: Positive. BAL culture: Histoplasma capsulatum identified on GMS stain. Antibiotics don't work on fungi — key clue that this was never a bacterial pneumonia.

hx_brief: 31yo female with 2 months of recurrent abdominal pain, bloating, and alternating constipation and diarrhea. Pain improves after bowel movements.

hints.hx: Symptoms began during a stressful period at work. Extensive prior workup — all normal. No blood in stool. No nocturnal symptoms. No weight loss. Symptoms have been present since college but were milder. History of anxiety and prior sexual trauma.
hints.pe: Mild diffuse tenderness without guarding. No organomegaly. Normal bowel sounds. Normal rectal exam.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Completely normal. Colonoscopy: Normal mucosa throughout. Biopsies: Normal histology. No microscopic colitis.

lab_results.Specialty: Fecal calprotectin: 28 mcg/g (normal — no mucosal inflammation; >250 suggests IBD). Fecal lactoferrin: Negative. Celiac serology: Negative. H. pylori antigen: Negative. Thyroid: Normal. Rome IV criteria met: recurrent abdominal pain ≥1 day/week for >3 months, associated with defecation.

hx_brief: 59yo male with 4 months of progressive bilateral leg weakness, muscle twitching, and difficulty swallowing. His speech has become more nasal. His wife says 'he seems to be fading.'

hints.hx: No family history of neuromuscular disease. Muscle twitching throughout his body — he can see his thighs rippling while at rest. Grip strength has declined significantly. No sensory symptoms whatsoever — crucial negative finding.
hints.pe: Fasciculations in bilateral thighs and upper arms. Tongue fasciculations. Hyperreflexia in all extremities. Bilateral Babinski signs. Dysarthria. Proximal and distal weakness. Critically: Normal sensation throughout — mixed UMN and LMN without sensory involvement.
hints.img: MRI Brain and Spine: No compressive lesion. Mild corticospinal tract T2 signal in bilateral internal capsules — UMN degeneration.

lab_results.Specialty: EMG: Active denervation (fibrillations, positive sharp waves) and chronic reinnervation (large amplitude MUPs) in 3 body regions — meets El Escorial criteria. NCS: Normal sensory conductions throughout — sensory sparing is pathognomonic. Confirms simultaneous UMN and LMN degeneration = ALS.

hx_brief: 26yo female with 2 weeks of debilitating fatigue, fever, and swollen glands. She developed a diffuse rash after taking amoxicillin for a sore throat.

hints.hx: Kissed someone at a party 3 weeks ago who was later ill. The fatigue is so bad she cannot get out of bed. No prior similar episodes. Her PCP prescribed amoxicillin 5 days ago for pharyngitis — the ampicillin rash that developed is a key diagnostic clue.
hints.pe: Temperature 38.8C. Marked bilateral posterior cervical lymphadenopathy. Tonsillar exudates. Splenomegaly 4cm below costal margin. Diffuse maculopapular rash on trunk.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Splenomegaly 16cm. No splenic laceration.

lab_results.CBC: Lymphocytosis — 68% lymphocytes with >10% atypical lymphocytes. Mild thrombocytopenia.
lab_results.Specialty: Monospot: Positive. EBV VCA IgM: Positive. EBV EA: Positive. EBNA: Negative — confirms acute primary EBV infection. Amoxicillin rash in EBV is immune complex-mediated, not a penicillin allergy. Avoid contact sports for 3-4 weeks — splenomegaly risk.
